articleTitle: FRIEND: Federated Learning for Joint Optimization of multi-RIS Configuration and Eavesdropper Intelligent Detection in B5G Networks

content.technicalOverview: This work presents FRIEND, a federated learning framework that jointly optimizes multi-RIS configuration and eavesdropper detection in cell-free mmWave networks using distributed Deep Convolutional Neural Networks trained on Channel State Information. The system employs collaborative training across edge devices without raw CSI data exchange, incorporating an early-exit mechanism to manage computational complexity. The approach combines physical layer security with intelligent surface reconfiguration to maximize secrecy rates while maintaining detection accuracy. Mathematical optimization targets the joint problem of RIS phase shift configuration and malicious user classification through distributed gradient descent on locally computed CSI features.
content.problemContext: Traditional centralized security mechanisms in B5G networks face fundamental scalability bottlenecks when dealing with distributed cell-free architectures and dynamic RIS configurations. The computational complexity of joint optimization across multiple RIS nodes while performing real-time eavesdropper detection creates latency constraints incompatible with Industrial IoT requirements. Previous approaches either treat RIS optimization and security detection as separate problems or rely on centralized processing that violates privacy constraints and creates single points of failure. The heterogeneous nature of edge devices and varying channel conditions further complicate unified security frameworks.
content.coreInnovation: The fundamental contribution lies in formulating eavesdropper detection as a federated learning problem where CSI patterns serve as distributed training data for malicious user classification while simultaneously optimizing RIS configurations. Unlike prior work that treats security and beamforming optimization independently, FRIEND creates a joint optimization framework where RIS phase shifts are configured to both maximize legitimate user secrecy rates and enhance the distinguishability of eavesdropper CSI signatures. The early-exit DCNN architecture introduces adaptive computational complexity, allowing resource-constrained edge devices to participate in training while maintaining detection performance.
technicalMechanics: Federated DCNN architecture processes local CSI observations to extract eavesdropper signatures without centralizing raw channel data, using gradient aggregation across distributed access points
Multi-RIS phase shift optimization employs alternating optimization between beamforming weights and detection model parameters, with RIS configurations designed to maximize CSI feature separability
Early-exit mechanism implements dynamic inference depth based on confidence thresholds, reducing computational load on resource-constrained IoT devices while maintaining detection accuracy
Channel State Information preprocessing converts complex-valued channel matrices into normalized feature vectors suitable for convolutional processing, preserving spatial correlation patterns
Secrecy rate optimization formulates the joint problem as maximizing legitimate user SINR while minimizing eavesdropper channel capacity through coordinated RIS reflection coefficients
Collaborative training protocol implements differential privacy mechanisms and secure aggregation to prevent gradient-based attacks while enabling distributed model convergence

productionConsiderations.scalability: The federated approach scales linearly with the number of edge devices, but coordination overhead grows quadratically with RIS nodes. Communication rounds required for convergence may become prohibitive with hundreds of access points, requiring hierarchical aggregation or clustering strategies.
productionConsiderations.latency: Real-time inference depends on early-exit thresholds, with detection latency ranging from milliseconds for simple cases to tens of milliseconds for complex scenarios. Training convergence requires multiple federated rounds, making adaptation to new attack patterns slower than centralized approaches.
productionConsiderations.resourceRequirements: Edge devices need sufficient memory for local DCNN models (estimated 10-50MB per node) and computational capacity for CSI preprocessing. No centralized GPU requirements, but coordination servers need bandwidth for gradient aggregation across potentially thousands of nodes.
productionConsiderations.implementationComplexity: Requires integration with RIS control systems, mmWave baseband processing pipelines, and federated learning frameworks. Implementation demands expertise in wireless communications, distributed systems, and deep learning, with significant integration testing across heterogeneous hardware.

content.applicationsAndImpact: This framework directly applies to smart factory deployments, autonomous vehicle communications, and critical infrastructure IoT where security and reliability are paramount. Production systems could achieve 30% improvement in secure communication capacity while reducing centralized processing loads and meeting privacy requirements. The approach enables secure Industrial IoT deployments in environments where traditional cryptographic methods are insufficient due to computational constraints. New capabilities include adaptive security that learns from attack patterns and distributed threat detection without compromising user privacy.
technicalPrerequisites: Deep understanding of millimeter-wave channel modeling and MIMO signal processing
Federated learning algorithms, gradient aggregation, and distributed optimization techniques
Reconfigurable Intelligent Surface theory, phase shift optimization, and beamforming
Physical layer security, secrecy capacity analysis, and eavesdropping attack models
Convolutional neural network architectures and early-exit mechanism design

keyTermsAndConcepts.definition: Programmable metasurface arrays with controllable electromagnetic reflection properties, enabling dynamic wireless channel shaping through software-configurable phase shifts of incident signals

Distributed network architecture without traditional cell boundaries where multiple access points coordinate to serve users, operating in millimeter-wave frequency bands for high-capacity communications
Information-theoretic measure of secure communication capacity, defined as the difference between legitimate user channel capacity and eavesdropper channel capacity, measured in bits per second
Complex-valued matrix characterizing wireless channel properties including amplitude, phase, and spatial correlation across antenna elements, essential for beamforming and interference management

content.architecturalInsight: The system architecture employs a three-tier hierarchy: edge devices performing local CSI processing and DCNN inference, access points coordinating RIS configurations and gradient aggregation, and a lightweight coordination server managing global model updates. Data flow follows a closed-loop pattern where CSI observations feed both the detection model and RIS optimization, with federated updates propagating security intelligence across the network without centralizing raw measurements.
content.limitations: The approach assumes CSI measurements are sufficiently accurate for eavesdropper characterization, which may fail under sophisticated channel spoofing attacks. Convergence of the joint optimization problem is not theoretically guaranteed, potentially leading to suboptimal RIS configurations. The federated learning process may be vulnerable to model poisoning if malicious devices participate in training.
content.comparisonToExisting: Compared to centralized eavesdropper detection systems, FRIEND achieves similar detection accuracy while eliminating privacy concerns and single points of failure. The 30% secrecy rate improvement over non-RIS methods demonstrates the value of joint optimization, though this comes at the cost of increased coordination complexity compared to traditional static beamforming approaches.
relatedWork: Intelligent Reflecting Surface-Assisted Physical Layer Security
Federated Learning for Wireless Communications Networks
Cell-Free Massive MIMO with Multi-Antenna Users
content.implementationNotes: Practical deployment requires careful calibration of RIS phase shift controllers and synchronization across distributed access points. The early-exit thresholds need environment-specific tuning to balance computational efficiency with detection accuracy. Integration with existing 5G infrastructure demands standardized interfaces for RIS control and federated learning coordination protocols.
